title: 2019 AMC 12A Problems/Problem 2
problem: Suppose $a$ is $150\%$ of $b$ . What percent of $a$ is $3b$ ? $\textbf{(A) } 50 \qquad \textbf{(B) } 66+\frac{2}{3} \qquad \textbf{(C) } 150 \qquad \textbf{(D) } 200 \qquad \textbf{(E) } 450$
solution: Since $a=1.5b$ , that means $b=\frac{a}{1.5}$ . We multiply by $3$ to get a $3b$ term, yielding $3b=2a$ , and $2a$ is $\boxed{\textbf{(D) }200\%}$ of $a$ .
